steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage concludes that invasives benefit more than natives when subfreezing temperatures arrive later in autumn.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A shows that both species leaf out later in warm years, but the invasive knotgrass extends its leaf period far more than the native dogwood, exactly the advantage the researchers concluded.

The other three either describe equal effects, introduce unrelated spring timing, or show no variation, which does not support the advantage claim.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
